title: The One With The Truth About London

jokes.text: Before we do, are any of Joey's special "romance" magazines in there?
jokes.setup: Simple request to use the bathroom
jokes.punchline: Ross euphemistically checking for Joey's porn stash
jokes.explanation: Solid character-specific humor - Ross being uncomfortable about Joey's lifestyle while trying to be polite

jokes.text: That's Rachel. But whatever.
jokes.setup: Ross correcting Phoebe about who thanked her
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's casual dismissal of getting the person wrong
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - her airheaded but confident delivery makes this work

jokes.text: Ben would be there.
jokes.setup: Rachel expressing concern about being alone
jokes.punchline: Ross's literal-minded correction
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective logical correction that reveals character dynamics

jokes.text: He's not an ex-con.
jokes.setup: Rachel nervous about watching Ben alone
jokes.punchline: Ross's reassurance using an extreme comparison
jokes.explanation: Ross's logical but absurd way of reassuring Rachel

jokes.text: I wasn't talking to you.
jokes.setup: Ross asking if someone will be okay, Rachel responds
jokes.punchline: Ross clarifying he was asking Ben, not Rachel
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with great economy - the audience assumes he's asking Rachel

jokes.text: Easy for you to say, you'd be wearing a veil.
jokes.setup: Chandler dismissing the spitting minister problem
jokes.punchline: Monica pointing out Chandler would be protected
jokes.explanation: Smart observational humor about wedding logistics and gender differences

jokes.text: I don't like the idea of me saying, "I do"... while he's thinking, "I'd do it too."
jokes.setup: Discussion of minister staring at Monica's chest
jokes.punchline: Monica's clever wordplay on wedding vows vs. sexual thoughts
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that's both clever and character-appropriate. Well-structured setup and payoff.

jokes.text: We're getting "married" married, not "6th-grade" married.
jokes.setup: Phoebe suggesting one of them perform the ceremony
jokes.punchline: Chandler's distinction between real and pretend marriages
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler-style dismissive humor with clear distinction

jokes.text: Does calling it not mean anything anymore?
jokes.setup: Monica rejecting both Phoebe and Joey for officiating
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's childlike belief in the power of 'calling it'
jokes.explanation: Pure Phoebe - her naive worldview creates perfect character comedy

jokes.text: And when I say legitimate, I mean gay... and in control of his saliva.
jokes.setup: Monica wanting a legitimate clergy member
jokes.punchline: Her specific requirements referencing earlier problems
jokes.explanation: Callback works but feels a bit forced. The gay stereotype feels dated.

jokes.text: Because you guys were on a break..
jokes.setup: Rachel explaining she's not with Ross anymore
jokes.punchline: Ben innocently referencing the famous Ross/Rachel debate
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback delivered through an innocent child - perfect show mythology reference

jokes.text: Fifty-two minutes.
jokes.setup: Ben asking when daddy is coming back
jokes.punchline: Rachel's precise, desperate countdown
jokes.explanation: Shows Rachel's desperation through precise timing - relatable parental anxiety

jokes.text: I'm funny, Ben, but I'm not stupid.
jokes.setup: Ben asking to do the quarter prank on Rachel
jokes.punchline: Rachel's self-aware refusal
jokes.explanation: Good self-awareness joke that shows Rachel learning from experience

jokes.text: Everyone knows I'm an ass man.
jokes.setup: Joey promising not to stare at Monica's breasts
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact declaration of his preference
jokes.explanation: Pure Joey - his casual, matter-of-fact delivery of inappropriate information

jokes.text: Internet ministers can have sex, right?
jokes.setup: Joey excited about becoming ordained
jokes.punchline: His immediate concern about celibacy
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey priorities - immediately worried about how this affects his sex life

jokes.text: Saran wrap on the toilet seat... so the pee goes everywhere.
jokes.setup: Ross confronting Rachel about teaching Ben pranks
jokes.punchline: The disgusting reality of the prank
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that's more cringe than clever, but serves the plot

jokes.text: I was barefoot.
jokes.setup: Rachel suggesting the prank was funny
jokes.punchline: Ross revealing the disgusting detail that makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that makes the prank much worse with minimal words

jokes.text: Just got off the Internet. Man, there's a lot of porn out there.
jokes.setup: Joey announcing he got ordained online
jokes.punchline: His inevitable discovery during the process
jokes.explanation: Perfectly in character for Joey to get distracted by porn while doing official business

jokes.text: It is a love based on giving and receiving... as well as having and sharing. And the love that they give and have is shared and received.
jokes.setup: Joey reading his wedding ceremony draft
jokes.punchline: His circular, meaningless wedding speech
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey - trying to sound profound but creating meaningless word soup

jokes.text: Should we call the spitter?
jokes.setup: Joey's terrible wedding speech
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dry suggestion they reconsider the bad minister
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler deadpan reaction that calls back earlier setup beautifully

jokes.text: I found him!
jokes.setup: Carol asking where Ben is
jokes.punchline: Ben jumping out to scare them
jokes.explanation: Standard jump scare prank, executed simply but effectively for the plot

jokes.text: Remember all that stuff I taught you yesterday?" / "Remember all that stuff I taught you yesterday?"
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to get Ben to stop pranks
jokes.punchline: Ben immediately doing the repeating prank on her
jokes.explanation: Good ironic escalation - Ben uses Rachel's own teaching against her immediately

jokes.text: Oh, damn it! / Damn it! / Go back to repeating. / Damn it!
jokes.setup: Rachel getting frustrated with Ben's repeating
jokes.punchline: Her swearing making it worse, then trying to fix it
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how pranks can backfire - Rachel making it worse

jokes.text: Only I won't say "hooked up"... I'll say, "began their beautiful journey... by doing it."
jokes.setup: Joey trying to be more elegant about describing their relationship
jokes.punchline: His attempt at class ending in crude directness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey - trying to be sophisticated but reverting to his crude nature

jokes.text: I call that "London style."
jokes.setup: Joey describing his fantasy of eyes meeting and bathtub strawberries
jokes.punchline: His made-up classification system for romance
jokes.explanation: Joey's tendency to create his own categories and rules for romance

jokes.text: You're the most beautiful woman in most rooms..
jokes.setup: Chandler complimenting Monica
jokes.punchline: His qualification that makes it more realistic but still sweet
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler - sweet but realistic, can't help adding the qualifier

jokes.text: Enough to know I want to do this. Not so much that you should feel guilty.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking how drunk Monica is
jokes.punchline: Monica's perfectly calculated response about consent and guilt
jokes.explanation: Brilliant line that's both funny and handles consent well - Monica's analytical nature

jokes.text: Well, I have kissed over four women.
jokes.setup: Monica complimenting Chandler's kissing
jokes.punchline: Chandler's awkward attempt to sound experienced
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler insecurity humor - the specific number makes it funnier

jokes.text: It bodes well for me that speed impresses you.
jokes.setup: Monica impressed by how fast Chandler undressed
jokes.punchline: Chandler's self-aware comment about sexual performance
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating sexual humor that's clever rather than crude

jokes.text: Well, I think it's safe to say our friendship is effectively ruined. / Eh, we weren't that close anyway.
jokes.setup: About to see each other naked
jokes.punchline: Their casual dismissal of their friendship
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan exchange that deflects the awkwardness with humor

jokes.text: For just you?
jokes.setup: Chandler asking Joey to leave him one condom
jokes.punchline: Joey's confused question about why he'd only need one
jokes.explanation: Pure Joey - his assumption that everyone has his stamina/appetite

jokes.text: Imagine if I had sent that hooker like I was gonna?
jokes.setup: Joey realizing his role in Monica and Chandler getting together
jokes.punchline: His casual mention of almost sending a prostitute
jokes.explanation: Casual Joey absurdity - treating hiring a prostitute as no big deal

jokes.text: Yeah, baby. / No, baby.
jokes.setup: Monica reveals she originally wanted Joey
jokes.punchline: Joey's excitement immediately deflated by Chandler's reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and contrast - Joey's ego boost immediately crushed

jokes.text: You wanted to sleep with Batman and settled for Robin.
jokes.setup: Chandler's hurt feelings about Monica wanting Joey first
jokes.punchline: His metaphor casting himself as the lesser sidekick
jokes.explanation: Excellent metaphor that perfectly captures Chandler's insecurity and the dynamic

jokes.text: No, Robin is so gay.
jokes.setup: Chandler's hurt Batman/Robin metaphor
jokes.punchline: Joey completely missing the point and focusing on Robin's sexuality
jokes.explanation: Joey missing emotional subtext entirely - though the gay joke feels dated

jokes.text: Carol... Lesbian.
jokes.setup: Ross asking for Carol's last name
jokes.punchline: Rachel's desperate attempt to fake knowledge
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment showing Rachel's desperation to seem close to Carol

jokes.text: Dude, don't you ever wash your face?
jokes.setup: Ross mentioning the line on his face from the quarter prank
jokes.punchline: The student's public humiliation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how the prank had lasting consequences

jokes.text: You want this scone? It came for me but it would rather sleep with you.
jokes.setup: Chandler offering Monica a scone
jokes.punchline: His bitter metaphor about being second choice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant extended metaphor that shows Chandler's hurt through humor

jokes.text: Honey, dinner's ready! / What's my little chef got for me tonight? / Your favorite. / Ho, ho, ho... fried stuff with cheese.
jokes.setup: Fantasy sequence of Joey and Monica together
jokes.punchline: Their relationship being all about Monica cooking Joey's favorite foods
jokes.explanation: Good character-based fantasy that plays to their established traits

jokes.text: How you doing?
jokes.setup: Joey's romantic fantasy with Monica
jokes.punchline: His signature pickup line in a domestic context
jokes.explanation: Classic catchphrase usage in unexpected context - the absurdity makes it work

jokes.text: You got something on your back.
jokes.setup: Rachel saying she can't do pranks anymore
jokes.punchline: Ben immediately tricking her with another prank
jokes.explanation: Good ironic timing - Ben gets her right when she's trying to be responsible
